name: What is the difference between a re-side and a siding repair?

mainEntity.name: How do siding repair and full siding replacement differ, and when is each one appropriate?
mainEntity.text: How do siding repair and full siding replacement differ, and when is each one appropriate?

acceptedAnswer.text: A siding repair is usually limited to a damaged area, such as a few boards, a corner, or a section around one opening. A full re-side removes and replaces most or all of the exterior cladding, often along with trim, flashing upgrades, and related wall repairs. Repair can be a smart option when the rest of the siding is in good condition and the damage is clearly isolated. Re-siding is usually the better investment when the siding is old, brittle, repeatedly leaking, or has widespread rot or cosmetic failure. In coastal communities, homeowners often start with a “repair” request and discover that hidden water damage makes a broader replacement more practical. The contractor should explain whether the problem is local or systemic and why their recommended scope makes the most sense.
acceptedAnswer.description: Siding repair fixes localized damage in a small section of the wall, while a re-side replaces most or all of the exterior siding system. Repair is appropriate for isolated damage; replacement is better when the siding is widespread, failing in multiple areas, or hiding moisture problems.
